task_completion.description: Weasis is open and displaying the CT image containing a bright central structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application window is visible, showing a loaded medical image (CT scan) with a distinct bright structure in the center.

task_completion.description: A line is drawn across the bright central structure using the line profile tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible line annotation (often colored or with endpoints) is drawn directly over the bright central structure in the CT image viewer.

task_completion.description: The line profile graph showing density is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D plot or graph panel is visible within or alongside Weasis, showing the pixel intensity/density corresponding to the drawn line.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates a screenshot capture of the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool (like a GUI application or a terminal command such as 'scrot' or 'gnome-screenshot') or a Weasis export dialog is visible on screen.

task_completion.description: The screenshot is saved to the exact required path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows the file being saved exactly as '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/line_profile_analysis.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Weasis GUI to generate the profile, not a terminal script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The line profile graph should be a native UI element of Weasis or a standard medical viewer, not a matplotlib window generated by a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The screenshot captures both the drawn line and the profile graph.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen area being captured (or the state of the screen when the screenshot command is executed) must clearly show BOTH the drawn line on the image and the resulting graph at the same time.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the image file rather than just creating an empty file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not use commands like 'touch /home/ga/DICOM/exports/line_profile_analysis.png' or copy an unrelated pre-existing image to the destination.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the screenshot is exactly /home/ga/DICOM/exports/line_profile_analysis.png. The CT image is a synthetic DICOM (PatientID: PROF001) containing a bright central structure.
